symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly growing firm crusted scalp nodule over ~6 weeks
symptoms.character: firm, raised, thick rough keratotic scale

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid weeks-scale growth of a firm keratotic nodule is characteristic of invasive SCC rather than a benign or indolent lesion.
BCC is typically slow-growing and pearly, not a rapidly enlarging thick keratotic nodule.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic UV exposure of bald scalp with surrounding actinic keratoses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cumulative UV exposure and field cancerisation (actinic keratoses) are the principal risk factor for cutaneous SCC.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central ulceration with active bleeding from a friable base after minor trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A necrotic, friable, centrally ulcerated tumour bed bleeds readily and fails normal haemostasis — a feature of an advanced invasive lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: New constant deep throbbing pain localised to the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain reflects tissue trauma/inflammation of the ulcerated lesion; non-specific but consistent with an actively bleeding tumour.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep induration with fixation to underlying fascia and exquisite tenderness on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fixation to fascia indicates deep invasion — a clinician palpation finding sealed from telehealth; the AI cannot elicit it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, matted, non-tender bilateral posterior cervical lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Firm matted regional nodes suggest nodal metastasis, upstaging the tumour — a clinician palpation finding, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Histopathology of full-thickness skin biopsy (pleomorphic squamous cells invading dermis)

investigation_details.expected_result: Pleomorphic squamous cells invading the dermis, confirming invasive squamous cell carcinoma
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without histology the diagnosis cannot be confirmed and definitive excision cannot be planned or staged.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histology is the confirmatory test — never available in telehealth; the AI can only route toward it, not obtain it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No faintness, dizziness or light-headedness (haemodynamically stable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Significant acute blood loss / haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pre-syncopal symptoms is reassuring against major acute blood loss and supports an urgent (T3) rather than emergency (T4/T5) baseline while keeping a bleeding escalation edge.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person wound assessment and mechanical haemostasis (alginate haemostatic dressing + compressive bandage)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Active bleeding from a friable lesion the patient cannot self-manage requires hands-on haemostasis and wound care that telehealth cannot deliver.
The only test that confirms invasive SCC and guides excision — arranged via urgent fast-track specialist referral, not performed by the AI.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Regional nodal assessment / staging (specialist-directed imaging and node evaluation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Firm matted cervical nodes raise concern for nodal metastasis; staging is specialist-directed after referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical systemic antibiotics without evidence of established infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: There is no confirmed secondary infection to treat remotely; empirical antibiotics are not indicated and would not be prescribed by the AI Doctor.
